title: Spring Boot Junior Developer Assessment
description: Junior-level evaluation focusing on Spring Boot fundamentals, REST API development, dependency injection, JPA data access, basic security, and common annotations.

questions.text: What does the '@SpringBootApplication' annotation do in a Spring Boot application?
options: It generates the main method automatically
questions.explanation: @SpringBootApplication is a convenience annotation that includes @Configuration (for bean definitions), @EnableAutoConfiguration (to auto-configure Spring based on dependencies), and @ComponentScan (to find other components/beans).

questions.text: Which dependency would you add to build a REST API with Spring Boot?
questions.explanation: spring-boot-starter-web includes Tomcat, Spring MVC, and REST support needed to build web/REST applications.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the '@Autowired' annotation?
options: To manually create a new object
To tell Spring to automatically inject a dependency into a bean
To delete a bean from the context
questions.explanation: @Autowired marks a constructor, field, or setter method for automatic dependency injection by Spring's IoC container.

questions.text: Which annotation maps HTTP GET requests to a specific controller method?
questions.explanation: @GetMapping is a specialized version of @RequestMapping(method = RequestMethod.GET) and is the preferred modern approach.

questions.text: What does 'application.properties' file do in Spring Boot?
options: It contains configuration settings like server port, database URLs, and logging levels
It is used for HTML templates
questions.explanation: application.properties (or application.yml) is the central configuration file for Spring Boot applications, controlling everything from server.port to spring.datasource.url.

questions.text: Which JPA annotation marks a field as the primary key of an entity?
questions.explanation: @Id marks a field as the primary key. @GeneratedValue is often added alongside @Id to auto-generate values.

questions.text: What is the default embedded database Spring Boot auto-configures when no datasource is provided?
questions.explanation: Spring Boot auto-configures an in-memory H2 database when it detects H2 on the classpath and no explicit datasource is defined.

questions.text: What does the '@RestController' annotation do?
options: It combines @Controller and @ResponseBody, automatically converting method return values to JSON/XML
It only works with XML responses
questions.explanation: @RestController is a convenience annotation that marks a class as a web controller where every method returns a domain object (JSON/XML) rather than a view.

questions.text: Which of these is a correct Spring Boot dependency for JPA and database access?
questions.explanation: spring-boot-starter-data-jpa includes Hibernate, Spring Data JPA, and the necessary ORM dependencies.

questions.text: What is the purpose of 'spring-boot-starter-test'?
options: To provide testing libraries like JUnit, Mockito, and AssertJ for unit/integration tests
questions.explanation: spring-boot-starter-test brings in JUnit Jupiter, Mockito, AssertJ, Hamcrest, and Spring Test utilities for comprehensive testing.

questions.text: Which annotation would you use to map a request parameter like '?userId=123' to a method parameter?
questions.explanation: @RequestParam extracts query parameters from the URL. @PathVariable extracts from URI template (e.g., /users/{id}).

questions.text: What does CrudRepository provide?
options: Basic CRUD methods like save(), findById(), findAll(), delete() without writing implementations
questions.explanation: CrudRepository is a Spring Data interface that provides standard create, read, update, delete methods. You only need to extend it, not implement it.

questions.text: What is the default HTTP port for a Spring Boot application?
questions.explanation: Spring Boot runs on port 8080 by default. This can be changed with server.port in application.properties.

questions.text: Which annotation marks a class as a Spring Bean for service-layer business logic?
questions.explanation: @Service, @Repository, and @Component all make a class a Spring-managed bean. @Service is semantically correct for service layer, but all work.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the '@Value' annotation?
options: To inject values from properties files into fields
questions.explanation: @Value("${property.name}") injects configuration values from application.properties or environment variables into Spring beans.

questions.text: What does the 'spring-boot-maven-plugin' do?
options: It creates an executable JAR, runs the app, and manages dependencies
questions.explanation: The plugin repackages the JAR to be executable via 'java -jar' and provides goals like spring-boot:run for development.

questions.text: Which HTTP method is commonly used to update an existing resource?
questions.explanation: PUT replaces an entire resource. PATCH is for partial updates, but PUT is the traditional full-update method.

questions.text: What does '@Transactional' do?
options: It ensures a method executes within a database transaction (all commits or all rollbacks)
questions.explanation: @Transactional declares that a method should run in a transaction. If an exception occurs, changes are rolled back automatically.

questions.text: What is the Spring Boot Actuator used for?
options: To monitor and manage the application via endpoints like /health, /metrics, /info
questions.explanation: Actuator adds production-ready monitoring endpoints that show health, metrics, environment properties, and more.

questions.text: Which annotation would you use to handle exceptions globally across all controllers?
questions.explanation: @ControllerAdvice allows centralized exception handling across multiple controllers. @ExceptionHandler inside individual controllers is local.

questions.text: What is the purpose of spring-boot-devtools?
options: To enable automatic restarts during development and improve developer productivity
questions.explanation: DevTools provides automatic application restarts, live reload, and enhanced debugging during development.

questions.text: Which dependency is required to use @PreAuthorize for method-level security?
questions.explanation: @PreAuthorize is part of Spring Security and requires spring-boot-starter-security plus @EnableGlobalMethodSecurity configuration.

questions.text: What is the difference between @Component and @Bean?
options: @Component is for class-level auto-detection; @Bean is for method-level explicit declaration in @Configuration classes
questions.explanation: @Component (and @Service/@Repository) are used on classes with component scanning. @Bean is used in a @Configuration class to explicitly create beans from methods (often for third-party classes).

questions.text: What does the 'Lombok' library provide for Spring Boot developers?
options: Annotations like @Data, @Getter, @Setter to reduce boilerplate code
questions.explanation: Lombok generates getters, setters, constructors, equals/hashCode, and builders at compile time, reducing verbosity.

questions.text: Which annotation maps a path variable like '/users/{id}' to a method parameter?
questions.explanation: @PathVariable extracts values from the URI template, e.g., @GetMapping("/users/{id}") void method(@PathVariable Long id).
